$schema: Inventory of Prisma models that intentionally do NOT have a row-level gate in lib/db-gated.ts. Each entry needs a one-sentence reason that survives review. Removing an entry is fine once the model gets a gate. Adding an entry means committing — in code review — that there's a non-row-level mechanism (route guard, scrubber, admin-only by construction) that protects the data.
User: Identity model. Visibility is controlled at the route boundary (admin/PM-only listing endpoints) and PII fields are scrubbed via lib/serialize.ts before reaching non-admin viewers.
Installer: Lookup data. All write endpoints under /api/installers/* are admin-only via requireAdmin(); reads are non-sensitive (installer names appear on every project). Pricing rows under InstallerPricingVersion are filtered by the schema-level @sensitivity admin annotation enforced by lib/serialize.ts.
InstallerPrepaidOption: Lookup data. Admin-write only via /api/prepaid-options/* route guards; option name reads are non-sensitive.
InstallerPricingVersion: Pricing structure. Sensitive @sensitivity admin fields (kiloPerW etc.) are scrubbed via lib/serialize.ts; reads are admin-only via /api/installer-pricing route guard.
InstallerPricingTier: Per-tier pricing rows under InstallerPricingVersion. Same scrubbing/route-guard rationale.
Financer: Lookup data. All writes under /api/financers/* are admin-only; reads (financer names) are non-sensitive and shown to reps on the deal form.
ProductCatalogConfig: Per-installer config. Admin-only writes via /api/installers/[id]/config; reads expose family lists which reps need on the deal form.
Product: Lookup data. Admin-only writes via /api/products; reads are non-sensitive (product names + family).
ProductPricingVersion: Pricing structure. Same rationale as InstallerPricingVersion — scrubbed by lib/serialize.ts, admin-only routes.
ProductPricingTier: Per-tier pricing rows under ProductPricingVersion. Same scrubbing/route-guard rationale.
ProjectCloser: Junction table. Visibility delegates to the parent Project — Prisma relational queries naturally inherit the Project gate when accessed via project.additionalClosers.
ProjectSetter: Junction table. Same rationale as ProjectCloser — accessed via project.additionalSetters.
ProjectCheckItem: Project-scoped child rows. Accessed only via project.checkItems include, inheriting the Project gate. No standalone /api/check-items endpoint exists.
TrainerAssignment: Trainer-trainee link rows. Read at the /api/trainer-assignments route which already gates by viewer (admin sees all, trainer sees own). TODO PR 4a — add a gate function for direct prisma reads.
TrainerOverrideTier: Admin-sensitive (trainer override payout structure). Currently read only at /api/trainer-assignments where the route is admin-only. TODO PR 4a — add admin-only gate so direct prisma reads default-deny.
Incentive: Admin-managed reward configs. /api/incentives is admin-only for writes; rep-visible reads only show 'their' progress via /api/data which already filters by repId. TODO PR 4a — formalize as an admin-only gate.
IncentiveMilestone: Per-incentive milestone rows. Accessed via incentive.milestones include, inheriting the Incentive boundary.
Blitz: Rep-visible (blitz events list). Visibility is currently 'all blitzes visible to all authenticated users' which matches the product intent — blitz signup is open. TODO PR 4a if visibility narrows.
BlitzParticipant: Rep-self-managed (a rep can join/leave a blitz). Read at /api/blitzes/[id]/participants which is open to authenticated users; participant rows are not sensitive.
BlitzRequest: Rep-submitted blitz proposals. /api/blitz-requests reads are admin-only via route guard; rep-visible state is exposed only through the bulk /api/data endpoint which filters by submitter.
AuditLog: Admin-only by construction. Read endpoint /dashboard/admin/audit is admin-only; writes go through lib/audit.logChange which uses prisma directly (intentional — audit must record even when the actor's gate would deny).
DataAccessLog: Admin-only observability surface. Same rationale as AuditLog.
